pattern: Two rectangles share a shape exactly when their width-to-height ratio matches, so reduce every rectangle's dimensions by their own gcd to a canonical ratio and count how many pairs land in the same bucket.
hints: Two rectangles are interchangeable when width divided by height is the same for both, which is the same as saying their reduced width:height fraction matches.
Reduce every rectangle's (width, height) pair by dividing both by their greatest common divisor, and group rectangles by that reduced pair.
Within a group of size k, the number of pairs is k choose 2; sum that over every distinct reduced ratio to get the final count.

edge_inputs.description: four rectangles forming several matching ratios
already-reduced and unreduced forms of the same ratio
swapped width and height are a different ratio, not interchangeable
extreme dimensions that both reduce to a square ratio
one ratio repeated three times plus an unrelated one

stress.description: large random rectangle lists across the full dimension range
